text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer, as it makes your work easier to understand and maintain. The first step is to write readable code, which means using clear names for variables and functions. For instance, instead of naming a variable "x," you could call it "userAge" or "totalPrice." Following naming conventions helps other developers (or your future self) quickly grasp what each piece of code does. It is also important to keep functions short and focused on a single task. This approach not only improves readability but also simplifies debugging and testing. Ultimately, clean code leads to better maintainability, saving time and effort in the long run.
